column1: ocal  visitor  numbers  to  key  Sri  Lankan  national  parks  (including  Yala,  Horton  Plains,
Udawalawa,  Eth,  and  Wilpattu)  show  a  pronounced  seasonal  decline  from  April  to  June,
starting at a high of approximately 40,000 visitors and steadily decreasing each month to just
5,000 by June. This sharp downturn, particularly noticeable after April, is strongly linked to the
factors such as school holidays and weather patterns. Yala National Park appears to be the
most popular destination, followed by Horton Plains and Udawalawa.
